skill.name: image-outpainting
skill.description: Extend existing images beyond their original canvas with RunComfy edit models. The skill helps choose a RunComfy route for uncropping, aspect ratio changes, and style-preserving image expansion.
skill.summary: Outpaint images with RunComfy while preserving the original subject and style.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from markdown code spans and prose. The real risks are the intended RunComfy CLI execution, outbound API calls that upload image inputs, and local token storage under the user config directory. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious behavior was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External RunComfy CLI Invocation
medium_findings.description: The skill grants Bash access for runcomfy commands and instructs agents to run the RunComfy CLI. This is required for the skill, but it can execute external tooling, submit image URLs and prompts, and download generated files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The markdown explicitly authorizes Bash(runcomfy *) and documents runcomfy run usage. The scope is limited to the RunComfy CLI, so this is an elevated operational risk rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Image Data Sent to External RunComfy Services
medium_findings.description: The workflow uses RunComfy hosted model APIs and references external image URLs. User images, prompts, and generated outputs may leave the local environment, so users should avoid sensitive images unless they accept the service boundary.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md states that the CLI posts to the Model API and allowlists RunComfy network endpoints. This confirms intended network transfer, although the endpoint is aligned with the skill purpose.

low_findings.title: Local API Token Storage
low_findings.description: The documentation says runcomfy login stores an API token in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. This is a normal CLI credential pattern, but it should be disclosed because it writes a secret to a hidden home-directory path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path and permission mode are stated directly in the security section. The storage location is legitimate, but credential persistence is still a privacy consideration.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Detections Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The repeated Ruby or shell backtick alerts correspond to markdown code spans, fenced examples, model identifiers, and prose. No Ruby execution construct or user-controlled shell expansion was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited areas are markdown documentation and example commands, not executable Ruby code. The only allowed shell surface is explicitly constrained to runcomfy commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Alerts Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts appear to match ordinary words, model names, prompts, exit-code prose, and workflow descriptions. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, host reconnaissance commands, or stealth behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited regions shows descriptive markdown, examples, and exit-code documentation. There are no crypto APIs or commands that collect system information.

dangerous_patterns.title: Permitted External Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill permits Bash(runcomfy *) and uses runcomfy run examples. Marketplace users should treat this as an external command skill with network side effects.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is explicit and intentional. It is constrained to a named CLI, which lowers abuse confidence compared with arbitrary shell execution.

content.value_statement: Cropped images often need more canvas for banners, product pages, or social formats. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to choose a RunComfy edit route and produce a style-matched outpaint.
actual_capabilities: Selects RunComfy edit models for prompt-driven, reference-driven, or brand-specific outpainting.
Provides CLI examples for extending images to wider, taller, or custom aspect ratios.
Gives prompt guidance to preserve the original subject, lighting, framing, and visible content.
Explains fallback routes for seams, mismatched lighting, or reference-style matching.
Documents RunComfy CLI setup, login, exit codes, output location, and service boundaries.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI, account access, and a valid token or interactive login.
Uploads prompts and image references to RunComfy hosted services for processing.
Does not provide mask-driven inpainting or local ComfyUI node execution through the CLI.
Output quality depends on the source image, prompt detail, selected model, and service availability.

use_cases.title: Convert Product Photos for Storefronts
use_cases.description: Expand cropped product images into consistent catalog frames while preserving the original product and lighting.

use_cases.title: Prepare Social Media Variants
use_cases.description: Turn square or portrait assets into widescreen, vertical, or banner formats for different publishing channels.

use_cases.title: Restore Cropped Editorial Images
use_cases.description: Use reference-aware outpainting to add plausible surrounding context to cropped photos or archival assets.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Canvas Extension
prompt_templates.scenario: A beginner wants a wider image with minimal changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Outpaint this image to a 16:9 canvas. Extend the left and right sides with matching background details. Preserve the subject exactly.

prompt_templates.title: Social Format Conversion
prompt_templates.scenario: A creator needs a vertical version for short-form video platforms.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this square image into a 9:16 vertical composition. Extend above and below with matching scene content and keep the central subject unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Reference Style Match
prompt_templates.scenario: A designer has a reference image for the surrounding style.
prompt_templates.prompt: Outpaint the source image using the reference image for environment style, lighting, and color. Keep the original visible area unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Step Quality Pass
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user wants controlled expansion with reduced seam risk.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend the canvas by about 35 percent on each side, inspect the boundary quality, then use the result for a second extension if the first pass is clean.

output_examples.input: A square product photo needs a wider ecommerce hero image.
output_examples.output: A RunComfy outpainting plan using a wide aspect ratio, preservation-focused prompt language, and a local output folder for generated results.

output_examples.input: A cropped portrait should reveal more body and background.
output_examples.output: A prompt that extends the lower canvas, keeps the face unchanged, and continues clothing, lighting, and background details.

output_examples.input: A banner needs the same visual style as a brand reference image.
output_examples.output: A reference-driven model route that uses the original image and style reference to guide the expanded canvas.

best_practices: Use only image URLs that the user explicitly provided for this task.
State the target aspect ratio and preservation requirements at the start of the prompt.
Review outputs for seams, changed subjects, or mismatched lighting before using them publicly.
anti_patterns: Do not send confidential images to hosted services without user approval.
Do not rely on vague prompts such as make it wider without preservation details.
Do not chain many large expansions in one pass when quality at the boundary matters.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an AI agent use RunComfy edit models to extend an existing image beyond its original canvas.

faq.question: Does it run locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the RunComfy CLI and hosted RunComfy model APIs for image processing.

faq.question: Can it change aspect ratios?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for square, portrait, landscape, vertical, and banner-style aspect ratio changes.

faq.question: Can it preserve the original subject?
faq.answer: It provides prompt patterns that ask the model to keep visible content unchanged, but results still require review.

faq.question: What credentials are required?
faq.answer: RunComfy CLI access is required through interactive login or a RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable.

faq.question: What should users avoid uploading?
faq.answer: Users should avoid sensitive, private, or regulated images unless they accept the RunComfy service boundary.
